title: Safaricom%20RPA%20case%20study
content:
RPA CASE STUDYSTREAMLINING TELECOM OPERATIONS WITH
INTELLIGENT AUTOMATION
At Safaricom.

Safaricom PLC is a leading East Africa telecommunications company, serving
over 45 million customers with innovative mobile, data, and financial services. As
a digital first enterprise, Safaricom is committed to improve customer experience
& operational efficiency through technology-driven transformation.
Safaricom partnered with Techno Brain to automate & improve key processes for
enhancing their customer experience and satisfaction. This case study highlights
how Safaricom automated its troubleshooting operations, achieving faster
resolution times and improved customer experience.42M customers,
6,230+ employeesCOM PANY PROFILE
Telecommunications
Industry
Presence in
15+ Countries
www.safaricom.co.ke 
02
Client : 

The customer had several processes that required employees to log in to
different systems to vet customer data, perform resets, carry out installations,
or run scripts to create accounts & reset radio equipment. Manual processing
for Engineers took an average of 4 minutes, while resetting radio equipment
required engineers to be physically present.
The Session Initiated Protocol (SIP) is a technical process used to set up and
manage phone calls and digital communications across telecom networks.
This process was time-consuming, prone to errors, often requiring multiple
manual logins and repetitive actions that led to delays and potential revenue
loss.
Deployed attended & unattended BOTs that logged into all systems and displayed
them on a single screen for engineers, reducing processing time by 60 seconds 
and freeing their capacity. 
For engineers, BOTs fully managed the whole process of resetting radio equipment
and handled new SIP creation, allowing engineers to focus on more meaningful
work.

onboarding.
Enabled faster and more accurate vetting of customers requesting services such
as SIM swapping, internet enquiries, and PUK (Personal unblocking key) requests.
Automation also enables tasks like setting up SIP trunks and resetting radio
equipment in areas affected by network outages to be fully automated, freeing
engineers to focus on other critical tasks.
